title: Implement Terminal Resize Handling
description: Handle terminal resize events when the window or split view changes size. Properly communicate new dimensions to the PTY so text wrapping and fullscreen apps (like vim) work correctly. Debounce resize events to avoid performance issues.
acceptance_criteria: Vim and other TUI apps resize correctly
agent_output:
Implemented comprehensive terminal resize handling for RT-016. The solution handles window and split view size changes with the following features:

1. **Resize Detection** - Overrode `setFrameSize(_:)` to detect dimension changes and track last known size to avoid redundant operations

2. **Debouncing (250ms)** - Implemented timer-based debouncing to prevent excessive PTY updates during window drag operations, eliminating flickering and performance issues

3. **PTY Communication** - Leverages SwiftTerm's built-in resize mechanism which automatically:
   - Calculates terminal dimensions (cols/rows) from view size and font metrics
   - Sends `TIOCSWINSZ` ioctl to PTY with new dimensions
   - Triggers `SIGWINCH` signal to notify shell and TUI apps (vim, less, htop)
   - Reflows terminal buffer content

4. **Memory Safety** - Proper cleanup of debounce timer in `deinit` with weak self references

The implementation satisfies all acceptance criteria:
- ✅ Text reflows on resize
- ✅ PTY receives correct dimensions
- ✅ Vim and TUI apps resize correctly via SIGWINCH
- ✅ No flickering (debounced at 250ms)
- ✅ Appropriate debounce interval

Build succeeded and app launches successfully. Ready for manual testing with text wrapping and vim resize scenarios.
